- deep-research: Deep research harness â€” fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly â€” if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium â€” an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, â€¦), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system â€” elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark â€” using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` â€” swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare â€” calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior â€” drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) â€” there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; highâ†’max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only â€” it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK â€” model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER â€” read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" â€” whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) â€” never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named â€” don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

175	roleCluster :: String -> SeDot a -> SeDot ()
176	roleCluster roleName dot = do

194	      | (gIdx, grp) <- groups, (mIdx, ru) <- zip [0..] grp ]
195	  where

197	      | isDestrRule ru                   = 0
198	      | isConstrRule ru                  = 2

235	-- | Dot a node in record based (compact) format.
236	dotNodeCompact :: Node -> Maybe String -> SeDot ()
237	dotNodeCompact node manualNodeColor = do
238	  let v = get nNodeId node
239	  (graph, colorMap, dotOptions) <- ask
240	  case get nNodeType node of
241	    SystemNode ru -> cacheState dsNodes v $ do
242	      let outgoingEdge = hasOutgoingEdge graph v
243	      let role = fromMaybe "Undefined" (getNodeRole node)
244	
245	      let rInfoVal = get rInfo ru
246	
247	      -- TODO this is pretty ugly. Shorten using standard functions
248	      let ruleColor' = case rInfoVal of
249	                        ProtoInfo protoRule ->
250	                          case ruleColor (_praciAttributes protoRule) of
251	                            Just rgb -> Just (rgbToHex rgb)
252	                            _ -> Nothing
253	                        _ -> Nothing
254	
255	      let color = M.lookup rInfoVal colorMap
256	          nodeColor = fromMaybe (maybe "white" rgbToHex color) (ruleColor' <|> manualNodeColor)
257	          attrs = [("fillcolor", nodeColor), ("style", "filled")
258	                  , ("fontcolor", if colorUsesWhiteFont color then "white" else "black")
259	                  , ("role", role)]
260	
261	      ids <- mkNode v ru attrs outgoingEdge dotOptions
262	      let prems = [ ((v, i), nid) | (Just (Left i),  nid) <- ids ]
263	          concs = [ ((v, i), nid) | (Just (Right i), nid) <- ids ]
264	      modM dsPrems $ M.union $ M.fromList prems
265	      modM dsConcs $ M.union $ M.fromList concs
266	      return $ fromJust $ lookup Nothing ids
267	    UnsolvedActionNode facts -> cacheState dsNodes v $ do
268	      lblPre <- (fsep <$> punctuate comma <$> mapM renderLNFact facts)
269	      let lbl = lblPre <-> opAction <-> text (show v)
270	      let attrs | any isKUFact facts = [("color","gray")]
271	                | otherwise          = [("color","darkblue")]
272	      mkSimpleNode (render lbl) attrs
273	    LastActionAtom -> cacheState dsNodes v $ mkSimpleNode (show v) []
274	    MissingNode (Left conc) -> cacheState dsConcs (v, conc) $ dotConcC (v, conc)
275	    MissingNode (Right prem) -> cacheState dsPrems (v, prem) $ dotPremC (v, prem)
276	  where
277	    hasOutgoingEdge graph v =
278	      let repr = get gRepr graph
279	      in or [ v == v' | SystemEdge ((v', _), _) <- get grEdges repr ]
280	    missingNode shape label = liftDot $ D.node $ [("label", render label),("shape",shape)]
281	    dotPremC prem = missingNode "invtrapezium" $ prettyNodePrem prem
282	    dotConcC conc = missingNode "trapezium" $ prettyNodeConc conc
283	
284	    --True if there's a colour, and it's 'darker' than 0.5 in apparent luminosity
285	    --This assumes a linear colourspace, which is what graphviz seems to use
286	    colorUsesWhiteFont (Just (RGB r g b)) = (0.2126*r + 0.7152*g + 0.0722*b) < 0.5

288	
289	    mkSimpleNode lbl attrs =
290	        liftDot $ D.node $ [("label", lbl),("shape","ellipse")] ++ attrs
291	
292	    mkNode  :: NodeId -> RuleACInst -> [(String, String)] -> Bool -> DotOptions
293	      -> SeDot [(Maybe (Either PremIdx ConcIdx), D.NodeId)]
294	    mkNode v ru attrs outgoingEdge dotOptions
295	      -- single node, share node-id for all premises and conclusions
296	      | get doNodeStyle dotOptions == CompactBoringNodes &&
297	        (isIntruderRule ru || isFreshRule ru) = do
298	            ps <- psM
299	            as <- asM
300	            cs <- csM
301	            let lbl | outgoingEdge = show v ++ " : " ++ showDotRuleCaseName ru
302	                    | otherwise       = concatMap snd as
303	            nid <- mkSimpleNode lbl []
304	            return [ (key, nid) | (key, _) <- ps ++ as ++ cs ]
305	      -- full record syntax
306	      | otherwise = do
307	            ps <- psM
308	            as <- asM
309	            cs <- csM
310	            fmap snd $ liftDot $ (`D.record` attrs) $
311	              D.vcat $ map D.hcat $ map (map (uncurry D.portField)) $
312	              filter (not . null) [ps, as, cs]
313	      where
314	        psM = do
315	          row <- mapM (\(i, p) -> do
316	            lbl <- renderLNFact p
317	            return (Just (Left i), lbl)
318	            ) $ enumPrems ru
319	          return $ renderRow row
320	        asM = do
321	          ruleLabel <- ruleLabelM
322	          return $ renderRow [ (Nothing, ruleLabel ) ]
323	        csM = do
324	          row <- mapM (\(i, c) -> do
325	            lbl <- renderLNFact c
326	            return (Just (Right i), lbl)
327	            ) $ enumConcs ru
328	          return $ renderRow row
329	
330	        ruleLabelM = do

332	          lbl <- if showAutoSource
333	                      then mapM renderLNFact $ filter isAutoSource $ filter isNotDiffAnnotation $ get rActs ru
334	                      else mapM renderLNFact $ filter isNotDiffAnnotation $ get rActs ru
335	          return $
336	            prettyNodeId v <-> colon
337	            <-> text (showDotRuleCaseName ru)
338	            <> (if null lbl then mempty else brackets (vcat $ punctuate comma lbl))
339	
340	
341	        isNotDiffAnnotation fa = (fa /= (Fact (ProtoFact Linear ("Diff" ++ getRuleNameDiff ru) 0) S.empty []))
342	
343	        -- check if a fact is from auto-source
344	        isAutoSource ::  LNFact -> Bool
345	        isAutoSource (Fact tag _ _) =not $ hasAutoLabel (showFactTag $ tag)
346	
347	        -- check if a fact has the label of auto-source
348	        hasAutoLabel :: String -> Bool
349	        hasAutoLabel f
350	            | "AUTO_IN_TERM_" `isPrefixOf` f  = True
351	            | "AUTO_IN_FACT_" `isPrefixOf` f  = True
352	            | "AUTO_OUT_TERM_" `isPrefixOf` f = True
353	            | "AUTO_OUT_FACT_" `isPrefixOf` f = True
354	            | otherwise = False
355	
356	
357	        renderRow annDocs =
358	          zipWith (\(ann, _) lbl -> (ann, lbl)) annDocs $
359	            -- magic factor 1.3 compensates for space gained due to
360	            -- non-propertional font
361	            renderBalanced 100 (max 30 . round . (* 1.3)) (map snd annDocs)
362	
363	        renderBalanced :: Double           -- ^ Total available width
364	                       -> (Double -> Int)  -- ^ Convert available space to actual line-width.
365	                       -> [Doc]            -- ^ Initial documents
366	                       -> [String]         -- ^ Rendered documents
367	        renderBalanced _          _    []   = []
368	        renderBalanced totalWidth conv docs =
369	            zipWith (\w d -> widthRender (conv (ratio * w)) d) usedWidths docs
370	          where
371	            oneLineRender  = renderStyle (defaultStyle { mode = OneLineMode })
372	            widthRender w  = scaleIndent . renderStyle (defaultStyle { lineLength = w })
373	            usedWidths     = map (fromIntegral . length . oneLineRender) docs
374	            ratio          = totalWidth / sum usedWidths
375	            scaleIndent line = case span isSpace line of
376	              (spaces, rest) ->
377	                  -- spaces are not wide-enough by default => scale them up
378	                  let n = (1.5::Double) * fromIntegral (length spaces)
379	                  in  replicate (round n) ' ' ++ rest

382	-- | Dot a normal edge.

389	                    maybe False p (resolveNodeConcFact src graph)
390	          attrs | check isProtoFact =

392	                    (guard (check isPersistentFact) >> [("color","gray50")])
393	                | check isKFact     = [("color","orangered2")]
394	                | otherwise         = [("color","gray30")]
395	      dotGenEdge attrs src tgt
396	    UnsolvedChain (src, tgt) ->
397	      dotGenEdge [("style","dotted"),("color","green")] src tgt
398	    LessEdge _ -> error "LessEdges are handled by dotLessEdge"
399	  where
400	    dotGenEdge style src tgt = do
401	      srcId <- getState dsConcs src ("Source node of edge not found: " ++ show src)
402	      tgtId <- getState dsPrems tgt ("Target node of edge not found: " ++ show tgt)
403	      liftDot $ D.edge srcId tgtId style

405	-- | Dot a less edge, which needs to be transformed first to contain the correct color.
406	dotLessEdge :: (NodeId, NodeId, String) -> SeDot ()
407	dotLessEdge (src, tgt, color) = do
408	  srcId <- getState dsNodes src ("Source node of less edge not found: " ++ show src)
409	  tgtId <- getState dsNodes tgt ("Target node of less edge not found: " ++ show src)
410	  liftDot $ D.edge srcId tgtId [("color",color),("style","dashed")]
411	
412	-- | Dot a legend listing all abbreviations by adding a sink node with a suitable HTML table label.
413	generateLegend :: SeDot ()
414	generateLegend = do
415	  (graph, _, dotOptions) <- ask
416	  let abbrevs = get gAbbreviations graph
417	  -- Skip generating anything if no abbreviations exist.
418	  unless (null abbrevs) $ do
419	    nLegend <- liftDot $ D.scope (do
420	      D.attribute ("rank", "sink")
421	      let sortedAbbrevs = topoSortAbbrevs $ zip [0..] $
422	            sortOn (Data.Ord.Down . render . Sys.prettyLNTerm . fst) $ M.elems abbrevs
423	          labelColor = get doAbbrevColor dotOptions
424	          htmlLabel = D.htmlLabel $ abbrevLabel sortedAbbrevs labelColor
425	      D.node [("shape", "plain"), htmlLabel])
426	    -- We add invisible edges from all sink nodes of the graph to the legend node to place it somewhere in the middle of the bottom row.
427	    -- We only add edges from the sink nodes because edges from earlier nodes will be routed avoid later nodes (even if they are invisible) and create constraints that lead to excessive whitespace on the edges of the graph.
428	    let sinks = getGraphSinks graph
429	    dotIds <- getM dsNodes
430	    mapM_ (\nsink ->
431	      case M.lookup (get nNodeId nsink) dotIds of
432	      Nothing -> pure ()
433	      Just nid -> liftDot $ D.edge nid nLegend [("style", "invis")]) sinks
434	  where
435	    -- | Render all abbreviations as a table using graphviz' HTML notation.
436	    abbrevLabel sortedAbbrevs labelColor =
437	      let tableAttributes = [Border 1, CellBorder 0, CellSpacing 3, CellPadding 1] in
438	        Table $ HTable Nothing tableAttributes $ map (renderLine labelColor) sortedAbbrevs
439	
440	    -- | Render an abbreviation to a table row in the legend using graphviz' HTML notation.
441	    renderLine :: D.Color -> (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion) -> Row
442	    renderLine labelColor (abbrevName, recursiveExpansion) =
443	      let cellAttributes = [Align HLeft, VAlign HTop]
444	          font txt = D.Text [Font [Color labelColor] txt]
445	          name = LabelCell cellAttributes (font [Str $ T.pack $ render $ Sys.prettyLNTerm abbrevName])
446	          eq = LabelCell cellAttributes (D.Text [Str $ "="])
447	          expansion = render $ Sys.prettyLNTerm recursiveExpansion
448	          -- The expansions can get pretty big, i.e. span multiple lines. To handle linebreaks we replace them with HTML <br> tags.
449	          expansionBR = LabelCell cellAttributes (D.Text $ expansion `joinLinesWith` Newline [Align HLeft]) in
450	      Cells [name, eq, expansionBR]
451	
452	    -- | Replace newlines in `s` with the given separator.
453	    joinLinesWith :: String -> TextItem -> D.Text
454	    joinLinesWith s sep = intersperse sep $ map (Str . T.pack) $ lines s
455	
456	    -- | Do a topological sort of abbreviations to ensure that we print abbreviations in the order in which they are defined.
457	    -- We define a partial order where for two abbreviations A & B, it holds A < B if A appears in the recursive expansion of B.
458	    -- To extend the partial order to all abbreviations we do a topological sort on the graph where the nodes are abbreviations and edges are based on the partial order.
459	    topoSortAbbrevs :: [(Int, (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion))] -> [(AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion)]
460	    topoSortAbbrevs keyedElems =
461	      let edgeList = map (\(key1, node) ->
462	                            let outlist = findLegendEdges keyedElems node in
463	                            (node, key1, outlist)) keyedElems
464	          (graph, vf, _) = G.graphFromEdges edgeList
465	          vertices = G.topSort graph in
466	      map (\v -> fst3 $ vf v) vertices
467	
468	    -- | We create an edge A -> B between two abbreviations A & B if A appears in the recursive expansion of B.
469	    findLegendEdges :: [(Int, (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion))] -> (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion) -> [Int]
470	    findLegendEdges keyedElems (abbrevName1, _) =
471	      mapMaybe (\(key2, (_, recursiveExpansion2)) ->
472	                  if isProperSubterm abbrevName1 recursiveExpansion2
473	                  then Just key2
474	                  else Nothing) keyedElems

480	-- to draw.
481	dotSystemCompact :: GraphOptions -> DotOptions -> System -> D.Dot ()
482	dotSystemCompact graphOptions dotOptions se =
483	    let graph = systemToGraph se graphOptions
484	        -- a.d. TODO make nodeColorMap filter systemnodes from graph instead of accessing System directly.
485	        colorMap = nodeColorMap (M.elems $ get sNodes se)

489	-- | Dot a graph in compact form (one record per rule).
490	dotGraphCompact :: DotOptions -> NodeColorMap -> Graph -> D.Dot ()
491	dotGraphCompact dotOptions colorMap graph =
492	    (`evalStateT` DotState M.empty M.empty M.empty M.empty) $
493	        (`runReaderT` (graph, colorMap, dotOptions)) $ do
494	
495	            -- Get the graph representation details
496	            let repr = get gRepr graph
497	                clusters = get grClusters repr
498	                edges = get grEdges repr
499	                nodes = get grNodes repr
500	                (lessEdges, restEdges) = mergeLessEdges edges
501	                abbreviate = get ((L..) goAbbreviate gOptions) graph
502	
503	            if null $ get grClusters repr then liftDot setDefaultAttributes else liftDot setDefaultAttributesIfCluster
504	
505	            -- Process the nodes, clusters, and edges
506	            mapM_ (\node -> dotNodeCompact node Nothing) nodes
507	            mapM_ dotCluster clusters
508	            mapM_ dotEdge restEdges
509	            mapM_ dotLessEdge lessEdges
510	            dotClustersEdges clusters
511	
512	            -- Generate legend if abbreviate is set
513	            when abbreviate generateLegend
514	
515	
516	-- Function to dot edges from multiple clusters
517	dotClustersEdges :: [Cluster] -> SeDot ()
518	dotClustersEdges clusters = do
519	    let allEdges = concatMap (get cEdges) clusters
520	        (lessEdges, restEdges) = mergeLessEdges allEdges
521	    mapM_ dotEdge restEdges
522	    mapM_ dotLessEdge lessEdges
523	
524	
525	-- Simple hash function to amplify differences between names
526	simpleHash :: String -> Int
527	simpleHash = foldl (\ acc c -> acc * 31 + ord c) 7
528	
529	-- Function to generate a value based on the role name
530	generateValue :: String -> Double
531	generateValue s = fromIntegral (simpleHash s `mod` 360) / 360.0
532	
533	-- Function to generate a color based on the role name with more aesthetic colors
534	roleColor :: String -> String
535	roleColor name = printf "#%02X%02X%02X%02X" r g b alpha
536	  where
537	    v = generateValue name
538	    -- Adjust saturation and value to get more pleasant colors
539	    RGB rf gf bf = hsvToRGB (HSV (v * 360) 0.75 0.85) :: RGB Double  -- Higher saturation and brightness
540	    r = floor (rf * 255) :: Int
541	    g = floor (gf * 255) :: Int
542	    b = floor (bf * 255) :: Int
543	    alpha :: Int
544	    alpha = floor (255 * (0.3 :: Double))
545	
546	-- Function to dot clusters
547	dotCluster :: Cluster -> SeDot ()
548	dotCluster (Cluster name nodes _) = do
549	    let baseName = fromMaybe "Undefined" (extractBaseName name)
550	    let color = roleColor baseName
551	    roleCluster name $ do
552	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("nodesep", "0.6")
553	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("ranksep", "0.6")
554	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("label", name)
555	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("style", "filled") -- Set style to filled to apply fillcolor
556	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("color", color)
557	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("penwidth", "2")
558	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("fillcolor", color) -- Use roleColor to set the fillcolor
559	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("overlap", "false")
560	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("sep", "4")
561	
562	        mapM_ (\node -> dotNodeCompact node (Just color)) nodes
563	
564	-- | Compute proper colors for all less-edges.
565	-- Computing the colors requires all less-edges of the graph, so we cannot do it per edge.
566	-- This is why we split up the edges here and use two different functions to convert them to a dot.
567	mergeLessEdges :: [Edge] -> ([(NodeId, NodeId, String)], [Edge])
568	mergeLessEdges edges = (merged, rest)
569	  where
570	    rest = filter (not . isLessEdge) edges
571	    merged =
572	      let lessEdges = mapMaybe extractLessEdge edges in
573	      map getAllRToC $ eqClasses (\(LessAtom x y _) -> (x, y)) lessEdges
574	
575	    isLessEdge :: Edge -> Bool
576	    isLessEdge (LessEdge _) = True
577	    isLessEdge _ = False
578	
579	    extractLessEdge :: Edge -> Maybe LessAtom
580	    extractLessEdge (LessEdge e) = Just e
581	    extractLessEdge _ = Nothing
582	
583	    getAllRToC :: [LessAtom] -> (NodeId, NodeId, String)
584	    -- SAFETY: Output of eqClasses never contains the empty list.
585	    getAllRToC [] = error "empty list"
586	    getAllRToC xs@(x:_) =
587	      (get laSmaller x, get laLarger x,
588	        -- Sort order is reversed to put the "most important reason" first.
589	        allRtoColors (sortBy (comparing Data.Ord.Down) $ map (get laReason) xs))
590	
591	    allRtoColors :: [Reason] -> String
592	    allRtoColors reasons =
593	      let len = length reasons
594	          per = if len >1 then ";" ++ show ((1 :: Double)/fromIntegral len) else ""
595	          colors = map toColor reasons
596	          scaledColors = map (++per) colors
597	      in intercalate ":" scaledColors
598	
599	    toColor :: Reason -> String
600	    toColor r = case r of
601	         Adversary      -> "red"
602	         Formula        -> "black"
603	         Fresh          -> "blue3"
604	         InjectiveFacts -> "purple"
605	         NormalForm     -> "darkorange3"

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Round-13 census: classify every ported edge endpoint by port-class.
3	
4	Info cell is the one whose text starts with '#' (a temporal var `#t : rule`);
5	prem/concl by column position relative to the info column.
6	"""
7	import os, re, collections
8	
9	CORPUS = "/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/oracle/dot_corpus"
10	
11	rec_re = re.compile(r'^(n\d+)\[shape="record",label="(.*)",fillcolor=', re.S)
12	edge_re = re.compile(r'^(n\d+)(?::(n\d+))? -> (n\d+)(?::(n\d+))?(\[[^\]]*\])?;$')
13	
14	def parse_record(label):
15	    """Return dict port_id -> class ('prem'|'info'|'concl').
16	    Walk top-level columns (depth-1 '|' splits); a port's cell text is the
17	    chars after '<nX>'; info column = the one whose first cell starts '#'."""
18	    depth = 0
19	    col_idx = 0
20	    # (col_idx, port, first_text_char)
21	    ports = []
22	    i, n = 0, len(label)
23	    while i < n:
24	        c = label[i]
25	        if c == '\\':
26	            i += 2; continue
27	        if c == '{':
28	            depth += 1; i += 1; continue
29	        if c == '}':
30	            depth -= 1; i += 1; continue
31	        if c == '|' and depth == 1:
32	            col_idx += 1; i += 1; continue
33	        if c == '<':
34	            j = label.find('>', i)
35	            tok = label[i+1:j]
36	            k = j + 1
37	            while k < n and label[k] == ' ':
38	                k += 1
39	            first = label[k] if k < n else ''
40	            if re.fullmatch(r'n\d+', tok):
41	                ports.append((col_idx, tok, first))
42	            i = j + 1; continue
43	        i += 1
44	    # info column = min col_idx whose a cell first char == '#'
45	    info_col = None
46	    for col_idx, tok, first in ports:
47	        if first == '#':
48	            info_col = col_idx
49	            break
50	    out = {}
51	    for col_idx, tok, first in ports:
52	        if info_col is None:
53	            out[tok] = 'unk'
54	        elif col_idx == info_col:
55	            out[tok] = 'info'
56	        elif col_idx < info_col:
57	            out[tok] = 'prem'
58	        else:
59	            out[tok] = 'concl'
60	    return out
61	
62	pos_style_class = collections.Counter()
63	files_with_info_src = 0
64	total_files = 0
65	info_src_edges = 0
66	info_dst_edges = 0
67	info_as_target_examples = []
68	info_as_source_styles = collections.Counter()
69	# also: for info-source edges, what is the target port class?
70	info_src_target = collections.Counter()
71	
72	for fn in sorted(os.listdir(CORPUS)):
73	    if not fn.endswith('.dot'):
74	        continue
75	    total_files += 1
76	    with open(os.path.join(CORPUS, fn), encoding='utf-8') as f:
77	        lines = f.read().splitlines()
78	    portclass = {}
79	    for ln in lines:
80	        m = rec_re.match(ln)
81	        if m:
82	            portclass.update(parse_record(m.group(2)))
83	    this_info_src = False
84	    for ln in lines:
85	        m = edge_re.match(ln.strip())
86	        if not m:
87	            continue
88	        srcn, srcp, dstn, dstp, attrs = m.groups()
89	        attrs = attrs or '(none)'
90	        scl = portclass.get(srcp, 'noRecPort') if srcp else 'NOPORT'
91	        dcl = portclass.get(dstp, 'noRecPort') if dstp else 'NOPORT'
92	        pos_style_class[('src', attrs, scl)] += 1
93	        pos_style_class[('dst', attrs, dcl)] += 1
94	        if scl == 'info':
95	            info_src_edges += 1
96	            this_info_src = True
97	            info_as_source_styles[attrs] += 1
98	            info_src_target[(attrs, dcl)] += 1
99	        if dcl == 'info':
100	            info_dst_edges += 1
101	            if len(info_as_target_examples) < 15:
102	                info_as_target_examples.append((fn, ln.strip()))
103	    if this_info_src:
104	        files_with_info_src += 1
105	
106	print("TOTAL FILES:", total_files)
107	print("FILES WITH >=1 info-source edge:", files_with_info_src,
108	      f"({100.0*files_with_info_src/total_files:.1f}%)")
109	print("INFO-SOURCE edges total:", info_src_edges)
110	print("INFO-TARGET edges total:", info_dst_edges)
111	print()
112	print("=== info-as-SOURCE by style ===")
113	for a, c in info_as_source_styles.most_common():
114	    print(f"{c:8d}  {a}")
115	print()
116	print("=== info-SOURCE edges: target port-class by style ===")
117	for (a, dcl), c in info_src_target.most_common():
118	    print(f"{c:8d}  style={a:34s} target={dcl}")
119	print()
120	print("=== info-as-TARGET examples (should be empty if source-only) ===")
121	for fn, ln in info_as_target_examples:
122	    print(fn, ln)
123	print()
124	print("=== FULL (position, style, portclass) census ===")
125	for (pos, style, cl), c in sorted(pos_style_class.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
126	    print(f"{c:8d}  {pos:3s} {cl:9s} {style}")

10	def features(path):
11	    with open(path, encoding='utf-8') as f:
12	        txt = f.read()
13	    feats = set()
14	    if 'color="purple",style="dashed"' in txt: feats.add('purple')
15	    if 'style="dotted",color="green"' in txt: feats.add('green')
16	    if 'color="darkorange3",style="dashed"' in txt: feats.add('darkorange3')
17	    if 'packmode="cluster"' in txt: feats.add('cluster')
18	    if 'shape="plain"' in txt: feats.add('legend')
19	    if 'color="blue3",style="dashed"' in txt: feats.add('blue3')
20	    if 'color="black",style="dashed"' in txt: feats.add('black')
21	    # info-port edge present? any edge nX:nY where nY is a middle-col port
22	    if re.search(r'n\d+:n\d+ -> ', txt): feats.add('srcport')
23	    if re.search(r'-> n\d+:n\d+', txt): feats.add('dstport')
24	    return feats, len(txt)
25	
26	files = [f for f in os.listdir(CORPUS) if f.endswith('.dot')]
27	# gather features + size
28	info = {}
29	for f in sorted(files):
30	    p = os.path.join(CORPUS, f)
31	    feats, sz = features(p)
32	    info[f] = (feats, sz)
33	
34	witnesses = ['01c5db0a7030e664.dot', '00082e1d6a47b5af.dot']
35	chosen = []
36	want = collections.Counter()
37	
38	def add(f):
39	    if f not in chosen:
40	        chosen.append(f)
41	        for x in info[f][0]:
42	            want[x]+=1
43	
44	for w in witnesses:
45	    add(w)
46	
47	# For each rare feature, pick the smallest files carrying it.
48	for feat in ['purple','green','darkorange3']:
49	    cands = sorted([f for f in files if feat in info[f][0]], key=lambda f: info[f][1])
50	    for f in cands[:4]:
51	        add(f)
52	
53	# add some small cluster + legend + blue3/black graphs
54	for feat in ['cluster','legend','blue3','black']:
55	    cands = sorted([f for f in files if feat in info[f][0]], key=lambda f: info[f][1])
56	    for f in cands[:2]:
57	        add(f)
58	
59	# a few small plain graphs for diversity
60	small = sorted(files, key=lambda f: info[f][1])
61	for f in small[:4]:
62	    add(f)
63	
64	print("CHOSEN", len(chosen), "files, total bytes",
65	      sum(info[f][1] for f in chosen))
66	allfeat = collections.Counter()
67	for f in chosen:
68	    allfeat.update(info[f][0])
69	print("coverage:", dict(allfeat))
70	for f in chosen:
